issue_title: Deleting a User or User Group might cause that all users suddenly get the permissions of the deleted user.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug


When a user is removed from keycloak, also a "User policy" selecting only this user is also removed.

If you have defined a permission which selects this policy and other policies with Decision strategy Unanimous. The user policy is bluntly removed, suddenly giving this permission to all users in the system.




### Version

22

### Expected behavior

User and User group policy is not removed, but after removal of the User or User group the policy always evaluates to false.

### Actual behavior

User policy is deleted from the database when the user is deleted. In permissions using this policy, this policy is removed.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a user "user1"
Create a client with Authorization enabled.
Create in this client a resource "resource1"
Create a user policy "user is user1", add only user1 to the list of users in this policy
Create some other policy, for instance time based "dayshift". Repeat. Hour min 8 max 17.
Create a permission "user1 has access to resource1 during the dayshift"
Add resource1 to the list of resources. Add policies "user is user1" and "dayshift" to the permission
Set decision strategy to Unanimous

Delete user1
Policy "user is user1" is deleted.
The permission only contains 1 policy anymore being "dayshift"
As a result, suddenly all other users are granted access to resource1 during the dayshift.


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Keycloak 21 included changes to the SAML SP metadata to require a different encryption key. To keep backwards compatibility with identity providers that were using the older metadata, the ```keycloak.saml.deprecated.encryption``` flag could be set.

If this flag is set, and an identity provider is updated to use the new metadata (and the new key for encryption), authentication fails and the following message is logged:

```
2023-11-09 09:55:08,616 WARN  [org.keycloak.broker.saml.SAMLEndpoint] (executor-thread-1) Not possible to decrypt SAML assertion. If keycloak is restarted without the flag set, authentication works as expected.
```

issue_title: 'Advanced Claim to Group/Role' UI Mapper Claims Can Incorrectly Save
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If you open an existing mapper of the type 'Advanced Claim to Group' or 'Advanced Claim to Role' or OIDC or SAML it is possible to save a mapper that can never match.

When a mapper is first created, it is possible to configure it to match for every authenticated user by leaving the list of claims and groups blank.

If a claim is added to the list, but then removed, the UI will add in a claim with a blank key and value pair, which will never match,

This can lead to user confusion when configuring the mapper as it looks the same in both the initial state and in the state where a claim has been added and removed - but the underlying mapper will behave differently.



### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

The UI should not store claims with blank key/value pairs.  I don't think its a valid use case.

### Actual behavior

The UI does store claims with valid key value pairs, effectively disabling the mapper, but without any clue in the user interface that this has happened.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Download the latest docker image (I am using FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:22.0.3)
2. Start in dev mode (for simplicity, I don't think this impacts the issue)
3. Add enough information to create a new IDP such that you can access the mappers screen,
4. Create a new 'Advanced Claim to Group' mapper, adding in at least one claim.
5. Click Save, navigate back to the **Provider details** page.
6. Open the same mapper for editing.
7. Add an attribute to the new mapper, but leave the key and value blank.
8. Delete the claim create in step 4.
9. Notice that it is not possible to clear the final claim as the minus icon is greyed out 
10. Save again, looking at the network tab, the following is saved against the claims: `claims: [{"key":"","value":""}]`
11. This saved mapper will not now match on login.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Removal EPL-licensed org.eclipse.sisu.inject dependency for CNCF compliance
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The CNCF org defines a list of licenses that are approved for accepted under their org. This list is to ensure that software within its projects adheres to legal and intellectual property standards that are compatible with its values.

Unfortunately org.eclipse.sisu.inject, a transitive dependency coming from quarkus-core-deployment is licensed under EPL and it is not part of the allowlist. If possible, we should remove it from our code base and in the worst case scenario, ask for an license exception.

## Scanner report

```
org.eclipse.sisu:org.eclipse.sisu.inject
Introduced through: [org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment
@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment
@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) › [io.quarkus:quarkus-core-
deployment@3.2.7.Final](mailto:quarkus-core-deployment@3.2.7.Final) › 
[org.eclipse.sisu:org.eclipse.sisu.inject@0.3.5](mailto:org.eclipse.sisu.inject@0.3.5)

EPL-1.0 license
```

### Version

>= 22.0.5

### Expected behavior

Distribution with less EPL licenses.

### Actual behavior

Dependencies with EPL license.

### How to Reproduce?

Check the [licenses here](https://github.com/eclipse/sisu.inject) and review the [allowlist provided](https://github.com/cncf/foundation/blob/main/allowed-third-party-license-policy.md#approved-licenses-for-allowlist) by CNCF.

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

User cannot update his profile (in "Update profile" required action or in account console) when there is some attribute, which is hidden to him (according to user-profile definition) and which has invalid value according to user-profile validation.

User should not be stucked because attribute is invisible to him and hence he should be allowed to proceed as he cannot update the attribute

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Allow user to continue login (or update his account in account console) when there is some invisible attribute on him with invalid value (invalid value according to user profile validations)

### Actual behavior

User is stucked when updating profile when there is some invisible attribute on him with invalid value (invalid value according to user profile validations)

### How to Reproduce?

1) Enable user-profile feature, but don't enable yet user-profile for the realm
2) Create user `john` with password `john`
3) Add attribute `foo` with value `abc`
4) Add required action `Update profile` to user `john`
5) Enable user-profile in the realm
6) Add definition for user-profile attribute `foo` with length to be between 5 and 15 characters (So attribute `foo` on user `john` is invalid now according to this defined validation). Make sure that attribute is visible and editable only for administrator
7) In different browser tab, login as user `john`. User needs to update his profile. Attribute `foo` is not shown on the update-profile screen, which is correct.
8) Try to update firstName and lastname and continue login. But I cannot continue login. After confirm update-profile, server returns 200 and it returns me back to the update-profile screen with not any message displayed to the user. So user cannot continue login, which is incorrect behaviour
9) In different browser tab, login as admin console and update attribute `foo` on user `john` to have valid value like `abcdef`.
10) Go back to the browser tab with user login (step 8) and confirm login again. Now it works and user can continue login.

User is not able to update his attributes in account console as well when he has invalid value of some attribute, which is not visible to him.

So it is clear that the issue is caused by the fact that user `john` has invalid value of attribute `foo` on his profile. However since it is not attribute editable for him, and he did not updated it in this request, he should be allowed to login.



### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Removal of LGPL-2.1 licensed org.jboss.metadata.jboss-metadata-web dependency for CNCF compliance
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The CNCF org defines a list of licenses that are approved for accepted under their org. This list is to ensure that software within its projects adheres to legal and intellectual property standards that are compatible with its values.

Unfortunately, org.jboss.metadata.jboss-metadata-web, a transitive dependency coming from quarkus-micrometer-deployment is licensed under LGPL-2.1, and it is not part of the allowlist. If possible, we should remove it from our code base and, in the worst-case scenario, ask for a license exception.

## Scanner report

```
org.jboss.metadata:jboss-metadata-web
Introduced through: org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT]([keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT)) › io.quarkus:[quarkus-micrometer-deployment@3.2.7.Final]([quarkus-micrometer-deployment@3.2.7.Final](mailto:quarkus-micrometer-deployment@3.2.7.Final)) › io.quarkus:[quarkus-undertow-spi@3.2.7.Final]([quarkus-undertow-spi@3.2.7.Final](mailto:quarkus-undertow-spi@3.2.7.Final)) › org.jboss.metadata:[jboss-metadata-web@16.0.0.Final]([jboss-metadata-web@16.0.0.Final](mailto:jboss-metadata-web@16.0.0.Final))
LGPL-2.1 license
```


### Version

>= 22.0.5

### Expected behavior

Distribution with less LGPL-2.1 licenses.

### Actual behavior

Dependencies with LGPL-2.1 license.

### How to Reproduce?

Check the [licenses here](https://github.com/eclipse/sisu.inject) and review the [allowlist provided](https://github.com/cncf/foundation/blob/main/allowed-third-party-license-policy.md#approved-licenses-for-allowlist) by CNCF.

issue_title: Mapper Option "Add to access token" Toggled Off Despite Claim Added to Token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

There was a change where the option "Add to access token" is being exposed in the admin UI, for at least the Audience Resolve mapper. Prior versions of Keycloak, this option was not available through the UI.

Since this change, there is some confusion because the option will display as toggled off in the admin UI, yet the claim will actually be added to the access token. The mappers are missing the "config" attribute for the option since they were created prior to this change. The backend services that process the mapper are handling this missing attribute differently despite what is being displayed in the UI.

This might be an issue with the "Add to token introspection" option as well.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

The behavior of the "Add to access token" option displayed by the admin UI should be the same as what happens when processed by the backend services.

### Actual behavior

Option is displayed as toggled off. Yet claim is added to the token.

### How to Reproduce?

We are upgrading from Keycloak 18 -> snapshots of nightly.

Create a Audience Resolve mapper on a client or client scope in a prior version of Keycloak.
Notice there are no options for "Add to access token" / "Add to token introspection".
Upgrade to the latest nightly version.
Notice that the options  "Add to access token" / "Add to token introspection" are now visible on the mapper. These options are showing toggled off in the UI, indicating that the claim will not be added to the token.
Go through the auth flow and notice that the claim is added to the token.

### Anything else?

Related changes: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/22148

issue_title: DefaultHostnameProvider reports being proxied when it is not
issue_body:
On the DefaultHostnameProvider log, that appears to be an incomplete fix from https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/10627 / https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/10707 - if kc.proxy is not not set, then false is being returned as the value, but if you explicitly set none, then none is being returned and noProxy is set to false.

cc @vmuzikar @pedroigor PropertyMapper.transformValue will return a ConfigValue representing the default where the raw value here is "none", but the mapped value is false.  This makes it inconsistent when explicitly using --proxy=none as the mapped value will be none.  It seems like there may need to be a change with the default handling, or the logic in the DefaultHostnameProvider will need to check the raw value instead.

_Originally posted by @shawkins in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/24294#issuecomment-1808653896_

issue_title: non-functioning session persistence when using JDBC over Infinispan
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Hello, I recently created a pull request regarding the issue of storing user sessions into the JDBC data layer through Infinispan. The PR also describes a use case.

PR link:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/24248

PR description:

The problem arises when the persistent jdbc layer is used to save the session. After logging in, the user session is only stored in memory and is not stored in the relational database as I would expect. However, in rare cases the saving works, but it was very accidental. After studying the integration code on infinispan, I discovered that the cache instance always returns with the SKIP_CACHE_STORE flag, which actually results in the avoidance of persistence. It complicates the use of keycloak for us, because after a restart, users are always logged out both on mobile devices and on websites. I have tested this fix and user sessions persist immediately after logging in. If you know another way to fix it, let me know, if there is no other way, it would be good to reconsider the purpose of the SKIP_CACHE_STORE flag. Well thank you.


I found the same problem on a forum as well. Also, there is a recorded response that describes the modification of CacheDecorators and a problematic flag. 

Here is link:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76661708/infinispan-doesnt-write-any-sessions-in-jdbc-persisted-cache-in-keycloak-21-0-2

Please try to look into it to see if it is correct behavior, thank you.



### Version

21.1.2

### Expected behavior

I expect that with each login, sessions will be saved to the database and will not be lost upon restarting KC.

### Actual behavior

Currently, the saving is random and in most cases, the session is not saved, resulting in users being logged out after a restart.

### How to Reproduce?

o simulate the problem, it is necessary to run KC with ISPN and JDBC configuration, for example on a Postgres DB, then perform a login and check the table where sessions are stored. I would perform the login multiple times with different users, as sometimes the session is recorded.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Not proper remove for nested sub-flows from DB
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In Auth flow in UI, when you delete one of the nested levels, the subordinates sub-flow is not removed from DB.
This behavior prevents re-creating sub-flows with the same name.
How we caught it, if you remove sub-flow from UI, you can't restore it with API (from Terraform code).
This is not related to terraform itself, because I can find these records in DB and error `{"errorMessage":"New flow alias name already exists"}` is correct

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

When you delete sub-flows on any level, all subordinated/underlying should be removed from DB, not only from UI/auth-flow

### Actual behavior

When you delete sub-flows on any level, all subordinated/underlying keeps in DB, but removed from UI

### How to Reproduce?

1 - spin up the smallest dev instance in the docker
[docker-compose.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/13377646/docker-compose.txt)

2 - login and create an auth flow
3 - create three-level nesting for sub-flow
inspect DB 
`SELECT * FROM public.authentication_flow
where alias like 'flow-a%'`

and can find all 3 sub-flows in the alias field and flow itself.
![2023-11-16_14-18](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/20001060/8a4dcfca-f16e-48bd-bca2-bc125266051f)

4a - remove second-level sub-flow
inspect DB 
`SELECT * FROM public.authentication_flow

![2023-11-16_14-12](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/20001060/07fdc1c4-76f8-4549-aaa9-aece2a5926de)

(repeat items 1-3)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/20001060/86b9c254-e786-48eb-9fd6-83b6c269dce2)

4b - remove first-level sub-flow
inspect DB 
`SELECT * FROM public.authentication_flow
where alias like 'flow-b%'`
and can find all 3rd-level and 2nd-level sub-flows in the alias field.
![2023-11-16_14-14](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/20001060/866e8a2b-d0d7-4d0b-8a25-83ea73bbc4f4)

removing flow itself, also not remove abandoned items

In version 22.0.5, however, no value (or, if set, the default value) is displayed, but not the configuration value that is loaded as JSON from the server. issue_title: Negative token expiration when changing client session max lifetime
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When adding a client session max limit, Keycloak will generate refresh and access tokens with negative token lifetime.

### Version

22.0.5, nightly

### Expected behavior

Keycloak creates a new client session and a new token with positive token expiration based on the client session settings.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak uses the original start date of the first client session and calculates the expiry date based on the now reduced client session max.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Do an authcode flow to retrieve an access+refresh token.
2. Enter a client session max of 1 minute in the realm settings.
3. Wait at least one minute.
4. Do another authcode flow to retrieve an access+refresh token, both tokens will have expiration date in the past and expires_in is negative in the response.

### Anything else?

This is really a corner case, probably not that critical.

issue_title: Error handling in admin console when update of user fails due the 400 HTTP error code
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When trying to update user and it fails with 400 error code, there is nothing displayed to the administrator in the admin console.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Some meaningful error message to be displayed to the user. At least something like `Failed to update user` . Even better some details from the underlying error.

### Actual behavior

Nothing displayed to the admin after he clicks `Submit` button to confirm the updates of some user

### How to Reproduce?

Reproduced it with read-only LDAP server.


- Start LDAP embedded server. Can be done for example by running:

```
cd testsuite/utils
mvn exec:java -Pldap
```

- Start Keycloak server by `./kc.sh start-dev` (Note that declarative-user-profile is not enabled)

- Create realm `test`

- Add read-only LDAP provider. Can be filled with the data like these when creating provider:

```
Vendor: Other
Connection URL: ldap://localhost:10389
Bind DN: uid=admin,ou=system
Bind secret: secret      (You can doublecheck "Test authentication" button if it works as expected)
Edit Mode: REad_only
Users DN: ou=People,dc=keycloak,dc=org
```


- Go to "User search" and search for user `jbrown`

- Click to that user

- Click to that user and try to change his First Name. Try to Click "Save"

- User is not changed, which is OK, but nothing is displayed to the administrator -> KO

In the last step, admin console sends PUT request to update user (something like PUT request to URL http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/users/54c745a1-6ba6-41ab-a29a-af7b5897ef4d ). This request returns HTTP error 400, which is correct behaviour as user should not be editable. But it's not good that nothing is displayed to the user.


NOTE: Same can be reproduced with declarative-user-profile feature enabled, but having it disabled for the realm


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OpenAPI spec doesn't match the admin API
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

This is a continuation of #23733 with two more problems in the generated OpenAPI spec for the Admin API:

- POST-methods returning `void` will return a 204 No Content response in the Keycloak API. This can be easily validated by looking at the browser's network trace in the Admin UI when, for example, adding a role mapping to a group. The OpenAPI spec currently defines these operations to return 201 Created though, which leads to issues in generated API clients. This is due to [this default in `smallrye-open-api`](https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-open-api/blob/23842c0c95a937ba2bc1fc5e7789d636a7e8fbac/core/src/main/java/io/smallrye/openapi/runtime/scanner/spi/AnnotationScanner.java#L545). The fix is to either manually add `@APIResponse` annotations with the correct status code to these methods as I've done in this PR or to change these methods to return a `Response` setting the status code to 201.
- Some API endpoints duplicate the `{id}` parameter in their path, for example `/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{id}/protocol-mappers/models/{id}`. This is fixed by this PR by changing some path parameters to be more specific, for example changing `clients/{id}` to `clients/{client-uuid}` to avoid the duplication and make it more obvious that the client's UUID instead of the client ID is required here.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

The generated OpenAPI spec should match the admin API.

### Actual behavior

The generated OpenAPI spec doesn't match the admin API and an API client generated from the spec runs into deserialization issues when calling API endpoints.

### How to Reproduce?


    Generate the OpenAPI spec using mvn -pl services -am -DskipTests -P jboss-release
    Generate an API client from the spec in services/target/apidocs-rest/swagger/apidocs/openapi.json
    Call the given API endpoints using the generated client
    Deserialization into generated types fails because the OpenAPI spec doesn't match what the API actually returns

### Anything else?

_No response_

We have a Keycloak setup where we have a SAML IdP configured. The IdP have added another signing certificate because the old one will soon expire. It is just a renewal, so the new certificate have the same public key. 

When adding the new certificate to "Validating X509 certificates" users are no longer able to login and get an "internal server error " message. Attached exception is logged. 

[Explore-logs-2023-11-23 10_30_46.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/13448012/Explore-logs-2023-11-23.10_30_46.txt)


### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

User still able to login.

### Actual behavior

User gets "internal server error" message and exception logget.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create SAML IdP. 
2. Add two X509 Validating certificates seperated by comma. The two certificates should use the same public key. 
3. Login through the IdP (fails with an error)

### Anything else?

I am not sure, but it might be related to the changes done in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/17609.

issue_title: The `DefaultActionToken` serializes a JSON Object with duplicate keys
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Performing actions that require a `DefaultActionToken`, such as reset credentials, verify email, update email, etc, generate a JWT as an `ActionToken` that has duplicate keys in the payload, therefore generating an invalid JSON.

The affected keys are those that have a `@JsonProperty(FIELD_NAME)` annotation in a method that calls `getOtherClaims()`.
I've identified the following fields:

- `DefaultActionToken` -> "asid"
- `ExecuteActionsActionToken` -> "rqac", "reduri"

I believe the reason is because in the `JsonWebToken` class, the method `getOtherClaims()` is annotated with `@JsonAnyGetter` and the JSON serializer will serialize all the `otherClaims` map one time, and then again the specific getter annotated with `@JsonProperty(FIELD_NAME)` which will read the values from the same `otherClaims` map

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

The encoded JWT in the `key` query param of the `login-actions/action-token` url should have a JSON payload without duplicate keys

### Actual behavior

The encoded JWT in the `key` query param of the `login-actions/action-token` url has a JSON payload with duplicate keys

### How to Reproduce?

Perform an action that generates this token.
For example, use the forgot password functionality in the login page, or use the admin panel to send an email to the user to perform an action in "Users > User details > Credentials > Credential Reset"

In the received email, copy the "key" query param of the link received.
Decode the payload part of the JWT 


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Client redirect_uri check must be compared using exact string matching
issue_body:
### Description

From Keycloak 21.1.2 as described in [this issue](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/24792), Keycloak has a bug with redirect_uri with uppercase letters.
This was fixed in version 23.0.0 in a bad and insecure way with [this PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/24819).

Maybe OAuth 2.0 allows case-insensitive redirect_uri check. Could you provide the specification part of it?
For sure, [OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-security-topics#name-protecting-redirect-based-f) mentions : 

> When comparing client redirect URIs against pre-registered URIs, authorization servers MUST utilize exact string matching except for port numbers in localhost redirection URIs of native apps, see [Section 4.1.3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-security-topics#iuv_countermeasures). We believe Keycloak should follow OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice. 

Moreover, this will be a step for implementing [OAuth 2.1](https://oauth.net/2.1/). : 

> The major differences from OAuth 2.0 are listed below.
>...
>- Redirect URIs must be compared using exact string matching

This issue could be a bug - or even a security issue, because it breaks [OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-security-topics#name-protecting-redirect-based-f).

issue_title: Bug: KC_DB_USERNAME environment variable is causing a crash in latest version
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When I build the following Dockerfile

```
FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest

ENV KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin
ENV KC_DB=postgres
ENV KC_DB_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/
ENV KC_DB_USERNAME=postgres

ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start-dev"]
```

And run it with the following command

`docker run -p 8080:8080 --network="host" -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin -e KC_DB_PASSWORD=password my-image`

I get a crash.

```
2023-11-24 14:11:36,335 DEBUG [io.quarkus.deployment.QuarkusAugmentor] (main) Beginning Quarkus augmentation

2023-11-24 14:11:36,582 TRACE [io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader] (main) Class quarkus.properties not found in parent first load from java.net.URLClassLoader@6af9fcb2

2023-11-24 14:11:36,582 TRACE [io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader] (main) Class quarkus.properties not found in parent first load from java.net.URLClassLoader@6af9fcb2

ERROR: Failed to run 'build' command.
ERROR: Cannot invoke "io.smallrye.config.ConfigValue.withConfigSourceName(String)" because the return value of "org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.mappers.PropertyMapper.transformValue(String, java.util.Optional, io.smallrye.config.ConfigSourceInterceptorContext)" is null
For more details run the same command passing the '--verbose' option. Also you can use '--help' to see the details about the usage of the particular command.
```

I used this Dockerfile throughout version 22 without issue, and when I specify 22.0 as the version, it works as expected.

When I comment out the KC_DB_USERNAME, I get an expected crash about user authentication failing for Postgres. If I add the variable in my run command instead of the Dockerfile, the same crash happens. Therefore I believe it's KC_DB_USERNAME causing the issue.

I checked the migration guide and release notes for 23 and could not find anything referring to why KC_DB_USERNAME is failing now, which is why I'm reporting this as a bug. The closest I found was the "[kc.sh and shell metacharacters](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/upgrading/index.html#migration-changes)" changes, but I believe that does not affect this environment variable.

### Version

23

### Expected behavior

The Dockerfile should successfully build and run.

### Actual behavior

The Docker image crashes when ran.

### How to Reproduce?

Use the following Dockerfile and connect it using your Postgres database

```
FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest

ENV KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin
ENV KC_DB=postgres
ENV KC_DB_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/
ENV KC_DB_USERNAME=postgres

ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start-dev"]
```

`docker build -t your-image .`

`docker run -p 8080:8080 --network="host" -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin -e KC_DB_PASSWORD=your-password keycloak`

Of course change `--network="host"` and `localhost` in the `KC_DB_URL` string depending on your platform. [https://docs.docker.com/desktop/networking/#use-cases-and-workarounds-for-all-platforms](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/networking/#use-cases-and-workarounds-for-all-platforms)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: NPE in checkAndBindMtlsHoKToken on Token Refresh when using SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor and Certificate Bound Token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using both SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor and Certificate Bound Token, checkAndBindMtlsHoKToken method occurs NPE on Token Refresh request.

Due to the issue, Keycloak 23.0.0 cannnot pass conformace tests (Test Name: fapi1-advanced-final-refresh-token) of Open Finance Brasil FAPI 1.0 (Open Banking Brasil FAPI 1.0 was renamed).


### Version

23.0.0

### Expected behavior

When using both SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor and Certificate Bound Token, checkAndBindMtlsHoKToken method does not occur NPE on Token Refresh request and returns a token response without a refreshed refresh token.

### Actual behavior

When using both SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor and Certificate Bound Token, checkAndBindMtlsHoKToken method occurs NPE on Token Refresh request.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Apply to a client Certificate Bound Token by HolderOfKeyEnforcerExecutor
2. Apply to the client SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor 
3. The client sends a token request with a X.509 client certificate and receive a token response with a certificate-bound access and refresh tokens.
4. The client sends a token refresh request with the X.509 client certificate
